rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH R. JANSSENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company Motion To Strike Complain
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 18, 2011, line 8, Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company's Motion To Strike Complaint. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF THE COURT'S RULING ON ITS DEMURRER. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH R. JANSSENS VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company'S Demurrer To Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 18, 2011, line 8, Defendant Pacific Gas And Electric Company's Demurrer To Complaint. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST-7TH CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO MEET THE BOCKRATH REQUIREMENTS. BOCKRATH V. ALDRICH CHEMICAL CO., (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 71. PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS LEAVE TO FILE A FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Allow Filing Of Third Amended X-Complaint And First Amended Answer To Secoind Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 18, 2011, line 1, CROSS-COMPLAINANT MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION Motion To Allow Filing Of Third Amended Cross-Complaint And First Amended Answer To Second Amended Complaint. DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A THIRD AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT AND FIRST AMENDED ANSWER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. MOST OF PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENTS AGAINST PERMITTING THE AMENDMENTS ARE CHALLENGES TO THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE PLEADING WHICH ARE MORE PROPERLY ADDRESSED THRU A DEMURRER OR MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. MOREOVER, THE PREJUDICE IS MINIMAL TO PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT. THE TRIAL DATE IS NOT UNTIL SEPTEMBER AND THE COURT WOULD ENTERTAIN A REQUEST FOR AN ORDER SHORTENING TIME TO FILE A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: UNITED BRANDS PRODUCTS DESIGN, DEVELOPMENT AND VS. MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify The Amount Of The Attachment Granted In Its Jul-16-10 Right To Attach Order For Attorneys Fees And Additonla Interest
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 18, 2011. Line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT MOSA INDUSTRIAL CORPORATION'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Modify The Amount Of The Attachment Granted In Its Jul-16-10 Right To Attach Order and For Attorneys Fees And Additional Interest. Motion to modify the amount of the attachment granted in the 7/16/10 right to attach order is Denied. Defendant/Cross-complainant has not made a showing of new facts. While Plaintiff/Cross-defendant's objection to the Vantress Declaration is well taken it is overruled. Defendants fear that Plaintiff is insolvent is not supported by the evidence which shows, if anything, that Plaintiff is not paying its obligation on time, not that they are not being paid. = (302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN JAMES BELISLE VS. DAVID A. SHERMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 18, 2011, line 2, CROSS-COMPLAINANT DAVID SHERMAN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES HAVE STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, EXCEPT IN SPECIFIED SITUATIONS (NOT PRESENT HERE), A WILL CANNOT OPERATE TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY DESIGNATED ON A LIFE INSURANCE POLICY. COOK V. COOK, (1941) 17 CAL.2ND 639, MOSS V. WARREN, (1975) 43 CAL.APP.3RD 651, 656. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT OF SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE FAILS. DECEDENT HAD 3 MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH TO CHANGE BENEFICIARIES. HE WAS ABLE TO CHANGE HIS WILL JUST PRIOR TO DEATH, YET TOOK NO ACTION TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY ON HIS POLICY. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE HFC LOAN AS THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THIS CONTENTION. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN JAMES BELISLE VS. DAVID A. SHERMAN et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 18, 2011, line 2, DEFENDANTS MINNESOTA LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY, INC., WELLS FARGO & COMPANY, ERRONEOUSLY NAMED HEREIN AS, MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED FOR SAME REASONS SET FORTH IN CO-DEFENDANT'S MOTION. [WHILE PLAINTIFF DOES HAVE STANDING TO MAINTAIN THIS ACTION UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW, EXCEPT IN SPECIFIED SITUATIONS (NOT PRESENT HERE), A WILL CANNOT OPERATE TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY DESIGNATED ON A LIFE INSURANCE POLICY. COOK V. COOK, (1941) 17 CAL.2ND 639, MOSS V. WARREN, (1975) 43 CAL.APP.3RD 651, 656. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT OF SUBSTANTIAL COMPLIANCE FAILS. DECEDENT HAD 3 MONTHS BEFORE HIS DEATH TO CHANGE BENEFICIARIES. HE WAS ABLE TO CHANGE HIS WILL JUST PRIOR TO DEATH, YET TOOK NO ACTION TO CHANGE THE BENEFICIARY ON HIS POLICY. FINALLY, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT BASED ON THE HFC LOAN AS THERE IS NOT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THIS CONTENTION.] =(302/LMG)
